scope.summary:
Goal: Implement code actions / quick fixes for the native code editor so an operator can act on a diagnostic instead of merely reading it. When the cursor rests on (or selects a range overlapping) a diagnostic line, the editor requests `textDocument/codeAction` from the attached language server for that range, surfaces a gutter lightbulb glyph on the affected line, and on click (or on `Ctrl+.`) shows a popup menu of available actions. Choosing an action applies its `WorkspaceEdit` to the buffer by reusing the apply path built in MT-048. This MT also wires the existing Editor Body Context Menu's quick-fix entry to the same request+apply flow. When no language server is attached (or the server returns no actions) the feature degrades silently: empty action list, no lightbulb, no menu, no error.

Expected diff shape: one new module `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/code_actions.rs` (~300-450 lines) that owns the code-action request lifecycle, action-list state, lightbulb decision, and the apply call; edits to `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/lsp_client.rs` to add a `code_action(uri, range, context)` request method returning `Vec<CodeActionItem>`; and edits to `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/pane.rs` to draw the gutter lightbulb, render the popup menu, bind the `Ctrl+.` shortcut, and route the Editor Body Context Menu quick-fix entry.

Exact files to CREATE:
- CREATE `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/code_actions.rs` ‚Äî defines `CodeActionItem { title: String, kind: Option<String>, edit: Option<WorkspaceEdit>, command: Option<LspCommand>, is_preferred: bool, diagnostics: Vec<LspDiagnostic> }`; defines `CodeActionState { line: usize, actions: Vec<CodeActionItem>, menu_open: bool, selected_index: usize, request_in_flight: bool }`; defines `CodeActionController` holding `Option<CodeActionState>` and the inbound result channel `std::sync::mpsc::Receiver<(usize, Vec<CodeActionItem>)>`. Exposes: `request_for_cursor(&mut self, lsp: &LspClient, uri: &str, range: LspRange, diagnostics_in_range: &[LspDiagnostic])` (sends `textDocument/codeAction` with a `CodeActionContext { diagnostics, only: None }`), `poll_results(&mut self)` (drains the channel into `CodeActionState`), `has_actions_on_line(&self, line: usize) -> bool` (drives the lightbulb), `open_menu(&mut self)` / `close_menu(&mut self)`, and `apply_selected(&mut self, buffer: &mut TextBuffer, cursors: &mut CursorSet) -> Result<(), CodeActionError>` which converts the chosen item's `WorkspaceEdit` into the same edit primitive MT-048 applies and calls the MT-048 apply function (do NOT re-implement WorkspaceEdit application).

Exact files to EDIT:
- EDIT `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/lsp_client.rs` ‚Äî add `pub async fn code_action(&self, uri: &str, range: LspRange, context: CodeActionContext) -> Vec<CodeActionItem>`. Send LSP `textDocument/codeAction` with `CodeActionParams { text_document, range, context }`. Parse the response array: each element may be a bare `Command` or a full `CodeAction` (LSP allows both) ‚Äî normalize both into `CodeActionItem`. If no server is attached or the request errors/times out, return an empty Vec (no panic, no error surfaced to the operator). Reuse the existing JSON-RPC request/oneshot plumbing added in MT-008; do not open a second transport.
- EDIT `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/pane.rs` ‚Äî (1) cursor-rest trigger: track cursor idle; when the cursor has rested ~300ms on a line and that line carries at least one diagnostic (read from the MT-007 gutter diagnostic store), call `controller.request_for_cursor(...)` for the diagnostic's range. (2) `Ctrl+.` shortcut: register `egui::KeyboardShortcut { modifiers: CTRL, key: Period }`; on press, request actions for the current cursor range and immediately `open_menu()`. (3) gutter lightbulb: in the gutter draw loop (next to MT-007 diagnostic glyphs) draw a lightbulb glyph on any line where `controller.has_actions_on_line(line)`; the glyph is a clickable `egui::Response` (Sense::click) that calls `open_menu()`. (4) popup menu: when `menu_open`, render an `egui::Window`/`egui::menu` anchored at the lightbulb (or cursor for `Ctrl+.`) listing each `CodeActionItem.title`; arrow keys move `selected_index`, Enter applies, Escape closes; on apply call `controller.apply_selected(buffer, cursors)`. (5) context-menu wiring: the Editor Body Context Menu (already present in the pane) has a quick-fix entry ‚Äî route its on-click to the same request+open_menu flow for the right-clicked line/range.

Exact crates / egui / AccessKit patterns:
- egui shortcut: `let qf = egui::KeyboardShortcut::new(egui::Modifiers::CTRL, egui::Key::Period); if ui.input_mut(|i| i.consume_shortcut(&qf)) { /* trigger */ }`.
- Lightbulb glyph: draw via `ui.painter().text(pos, Align2::CENTER_CENTER, "\u{1F4A1}", FontId::proportional(13.0), visuals.warn_fg_color)` OR an `egui::Image` of a bundled lightbulb svg; wrap in `ui.interact(rect, id, Sense::click())` for the click target.
- Menu: prefer `egui::Window::new("code_action_menu").fixed_pos(anchor_px).show(ctx, |ui| { for (i, a) in actions.iter().enumerate() { if ui.selectable_label(i==sel, &a.title).clicked() { ... } } })`.
- AccessKit: the popup menu container MUST expose `author_id = "code_editor_quickfix_menu"` with `Role::Menu`; each action row should expose `author_id = "code_editor_quickfix_item_{i}"` with `Role::MenuItem`; the gutter lightbulb should expose `author_id = "code_editor_quickfix_lightbulb_{line}"` with `Role::Button`. Use the WP-011 AccessKit id registry helper at `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/registry.rs` (do not hand-roll id strings outside the registry).
- Reuse WP-011 shell primitives: pane registry (`src/frontend/handshake_native/src/pane_registry.rs` ‚Äî the code editor is a registered pane; obtain its pane id from the registry), command bus (`src/frontend/handshake_native/src/command_registry.rs` + `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/event_bus.rs` ‚Äî register a `code-editor.quick-fix` command so the command palette and swarm agents can trigger it), and the AccessKit id registry (`src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/registry.rs` ‚Äî all author_ids above flow through it).

Exact handshake_core API bound: NONE directly. Code actions come from the LSP server over stdio (the same transport MT-008 established). There is no new handshake_core HTTP/Tauri endpoint for this MT. If a future server-side code-action source is desired it is a separate typed blocker, not in scope here.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
1. Add `CodeActionItem`, `CodeActionContext`, `CodeActionParams`, and `LspCommand` types (lsp-types already provides `CodeAction`, `Command`, `CodeActionContext`, `CodeActionParams`, `CodeActionOrCommand` from MT-008's `lsp-types` dependency ‚Äî prefer reusing those over redefining).
2. In `lsp_client.rs`, implement `code_action` mirroring the existing `completion`/`hover` request pattern from MT-008: build params, send JSON-RPC, await the oneshot, deserialize into `Vec<CodeActionOrCommand>`, normalize to `Vec<CodeActionItem>`. Bare `Command` -> item with `command: Some(..)`, `edit: None`. Full `CodeAction` -> item carrying `edit`, `command`, `is_preferred`, `diagnostics`.
3. The cursor-rest trigger must read diagnostics from the MT-007 gutter diagnostic store (the same store `push_diagnostics` writes to). Only request code actions when the rested line has >=1 diagnostic, to avoid spamming the server on every idle frame.
4. `request_for_cursor` runs the async LSP call off the egui frame thread (tokio spawn) and returns results via the controller's mpsc channel; `poll_results` drains the channel each frame (same non-blocking pattern MT-008 uses for completion).
5. `apply_selected`: take the chosen `CodeActionItem.edit` (a `WorkspaceEdit`), convert each `TextEdit` into the buffer-edit primitive MT-048 already applies, and call the MT-048 apply function. If the item has only a `command` and no `edit`, send `workspace/executeCommand` via the LSP client (server then pushes a `workspace/applyEdit` which the MT-048 apply path also handles) ‚Äî if `workspace/executeCommand` is unavailable, no-op gracefully.
6. Lightbulb visibility: draw only when `has_actions_on_line(line)` is true AND the menu is closed; while the menu is open, keep the lightbulb highlighted.
7. Degradation: if `lsp_client` is `None`, `request_for_cursor` short-circuits to an empty action list, `has_actions_on_line` returns false, no lightbulb is drawn, `Ctrl+.` opens an empty menu that immediately reports 'No quick fixes available' and closes ‚Äî never panics, never errors.
8. Wire the existing Editor Body Context Menu quick-fix entry: locate the context-menu builder in `pane.rs` (the menu already lists Cut/Copy/Paste etc.), add/route the 'Quick Fix...' item to call the same request_for_cursor + open_menu flow scoped to the clicked line.

Dependencies / unblock conditions:
- MT-007 (gutter diagnostics + `push_diagnostics` store) must be merged ‚Äî the cursor-rest trigger and lightbulb depend on knowing which lines carry diagnostics.
- MT-008 (LSP client over stdio with JSON-RPC plumbing and lsp-types) must be merged ‚Äî `code_action` reuses that transport.
- MT-048 (WorkspaceEdit / multi-edit apply path) must be merged ‚Äî `apply_selected` reuses its apply function and must NOT re-implement it.
- Blocks MT-044 (which depends on quick-fix being available as part of the act-on-error workflow).

HARD constraints: PostgreSQL/EventLedger only (no SQLite anywhere); no backend rewrite ‚Äî bind existing handshake_core APIs only and treat gaps as typed blockers (this MT binds NONE and that is correct); egui + AccessKit native (no React, no webview); reuse WP-011 shell primitives (pane registry `pane_registry.rs`, command bus `command_registry.rs` + `event_bus.rs`, AccessKit id registry `accessibility/registry.rs`).

Risk if missed: The quick-fix lightbulb is absent, diagnostics remain read-only, and the act-on-error workflow is broken versus VS Code ‚Äî operators see errors but cannot apply the server's suggested fix from the editor, forcing manual edits and breaking the parity goal.
acceptance_criteria: AC-001: `code_action` request: with a mock LSP server returning 2 quick-fixes for a given diagnostic range, `LspClient::code_action(uri, range, context)` returns a Vec of 2 normalized CodeActionItem values (titles preserved, edit present) ‚Äî proven by `cargo test code_action_request`.

AC-002: `code_action` apply: selecting an action whose WorkspaceEdit replaces a token results in the buffer containing the replacement text after `apply_selected`, applied via the MT-048 apply path (not a re-implementation) ‚Äî proven by `cargo test code_action_apply`.

AC-003: Gutter lightbulb appears on a line that carries a diagnostic with available actions and does NOT appear on lines without actions ‚Äî proven by an egui_kittest screenshot showing the lightbulb glyph on the diagnostic line.

AC-004: The popup menu container is exposed in the AccessKit tree with author_id='code_editor_quickfix_menu' and role=Menu, with at least one child menu item ‚Äî proven by an egui_kittest AccessKit tree dump.

AC-005: `Ctrl+.` opens the quick-fix menu at the cursor; when actions exist they are listed and selectable, and when none exist the menu degrades gracefully (no panic) ‚Äî proven by an egui_kittest interaction test.

AC-006: Degradation: with no LSP client attached, no lightbulb is drawn, `has_actions_on_line` returns false for all lines, and `Ctrl+.` does not panic ‚Äî proven by a unit/kittest test with `lsp_client = None`.

AC-007: The Editor Body Context Menu quick-fix entry routes to the same request+open_menu flow (no duplicate apply logic) ‚Äî proven by grep of the context-menu handler calling the shared controller method, plus a kittest confirming the menu opens from the context-menu entry.
AC-008: `cargo test -p handshake-native code_action` passes with no failures and no panics.
proof_targets: PT-001: Run `cargo test -p handshake-native code_action_request -- --nocapture` (mock LSP returns 2 quick-fixes for a diagnostic range) => 2 CodeActionItem returned, test GREEN.
PT-002: Run `cargo test -p handshake-native code_action_apply -- --nocapture` (selected action WorkspaceEdit applied to buffer via MT-048 apply path) => buffer reflects edit, test GREEN.
PT-003: Run egui_kittest screenshot test `code_action_lightbulb_on_diagnostic` => screenshot saved to `test_output/MT-049-lightbulb.png`, lightbulb glyph present on the diagnostic line.
PT-004: Run egui_kittest AccessKit dump test `code_action_menu_accesskit` => JSON contains author_id='code_editor_quickfix_menu' with role=Menu and >=1 menu item child.
PT-005: Run egui_kittest interaction test `code_action_ctrl_period_and_degrade` => Ctrl+. opens menu with actions when LSP present; with lsp_client=None no lightbulb and no panic.
implementation_notes: Reuse lsp-types `CodeAction`, `Command`, `CodeActionContext`, `CodeActionParams`, `CodeActionOrCommand` from the MT-008 dependency rather than defining parallel structs; `CodeActionItem` is just the normalized internal projection.
Do NOT re-implement WorkspaceEdit application ‚Äî call the MT-048 apply function. `apply_selected` converts the chosen item's edit into MT-048's edit primitive and delegates.
Gate the cursor-rest code-action request behind 'line has >=1 diagnostic' (read MT-007's diagnostic store) so the server is not queried every idle frame.
Run the LSP `code_action` call off the egui frame thread (tokio spawn) and return results via mpsc, draining in `poll_results` each frame ‚Äî identical non-blocking pattern to MT-008 completion.

All author_ids ('code_editor_quickfix_menu', 'code_editor_quickfix_item_{i}', 'code_editor_quickfix_lightbulb_{line}') must be allocated through the WP-011 AccessKit id registry at `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/registry.rs`, not hand-built string literals.

Register a `code-editor.quick-fix` command on the WP-011 command bus (`src/frontend/handshake_native/src/command_registry.rs` + `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/event_bus.rs`) so the command palette (`src/frontend/handshake_native/src/command_palette.rs`) and swarm agents can trigger quick-fix without the gutter click.
Keep the lightbulb glyph theme-aware (use `visuals.warn_fg_color` or a registered theme token via `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/theme/`), not a hardcoded color.

Cross-WP dependency: MT-007, MT-008, and MT-048 are sibling WP-KERNEL-012 microtasks; the reused shell primitives (pane registry, command bus, event bus, AccessKit id registry, theme, command palette) are delivered by WP-KERNEL-011 and live as flat modules/subdirs under `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/`.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/operator/ProblemsView.tsx ‚Äî diagnostic-to-action linkage: how a diagnostic row maps to its available quick-fix actions and how selecting an action triggers the edit; port the linkage model into CodeActionController, not the React rendering.
binds_backend_api: None ‚Äî code actions are sourced from the attached LSP server via `textDocument/codeAction` over the existing MT-008 stdio JSON-RPC transport. No new handshake_core HTTP/Tauri endpoint is bound; any server-side code-action source would be a separate typed blocker, out of scope.

risks: RISK-001: Cursor-rest trigger fires `textDocument/codeAction` on every idle frame, flooding the language server and causing UI jank or server backpressure.
RISK-002: Re-implementing WorkspaceEdit application instead of reusing MT-048 diverges from the canonical apply path, causing inconsistent edits, broken undo grouping, or off-by-one ranges.
RISK-003: A code action that returns only a `command` (no `edit`) is silently dropped, so server-driven fixes (executeCommand -> applyEdit) never apply.
RISK-004: Lightbulb / menu AccessKit author_ids collide when multiple code-editor panes are open, breaking swarm-agent addressing and kittest dumps.
RISK-005: Applying a WorkspaceEdit that targets a different file than the active buffer is mishandled (only same-file edits applied), losing multi-file fixes.
RISK-006: `Ctrl+.` is consumed by the bus/another widget before reaching the code editor, so the shortcut never triggers; or it triggers with no LSP and panics instead of degrading.
RISK-007: Stale action list ‚Äî actions requested for line N are applied after the buffer changed, so ranges no longer match and the edit corrupts the document.
minimum_controls: MC-001: Debounce the cursor-rest request (~300ms idle) and only request when the rested line carries >=1 diagnostic; cancel/ignore in-flight results if the cursor moved lines.
MC-002: `apply_selected` MUST delegate to the MT-048 apply function; add a test asserting the MT-048 apply entry point is the call site (grep + unit test), no parallel apply code.
MC-003: Items with only a `command` route through `workspace/executeCommand`; if unsupported, no-op gracefully and never drop silently without the graceful path ‚Äî covered by a unit test.
MC-004: Allocate all lightbulb/menu/item author_ids through the WP-011 AccessKit id registry with the pane id as a scope suffix so multi-pane instances do not collide.
MC-005: Guard multi-file WorkspaceEdits: apply same-file edits and surface a typed blocker/log for cross-file edits rather than silently dropping them.
MC-006: Register `Ctrl+.` via the command bus with explicit `consume_shortcut` in the code-editor pane; test the no-LSP path opens an empty menu and never panics.
MC-007: Tag each action list with the buffer version/line it was requested for; reject `apply_selected` if the buffer changed since the request, re-requesting instead of applying stale ranges.
